name: March Madness
description: The NCAA Division I Men's Basketball Tournament — Selection Sunday to One Shining Moment.

questions.q: In what year was the first NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament held?

source.notes: Eight teams competed in the inaugural tournament.

questions.q: Which team won the very first NCAA men's basketball tournament in 1939?

source.notes: The Oregon Webfoots (now Ducks) beat Ohio State.

questions.q: Which university has won the most NCAA men's basketball championships?

source.notes: 11 titles, including 10 under coach John Wooden.

questions.q: Which legendary coach won 10 NCAA championships at UCLA, including 7 in a row?

source.notes: Wooden won titles 1964–65, 1967–73, 1975. 'The Wizard of Westwood'.

questions.q: In what year did the NCAA tournament expand to its modern 64-team format?

source.notes: Villanova won that very tournament as an 8-seed.

questions.q: How many teams compete in the current NCAA tournament format (since 2011)?

source.notes: Adds the four 'First Four' play-in games before the round of 64.

questions.q: What is the name of the first round of play-in games introduced in 2011?

source.notes: Eight teams compete for four spots.

questions.q: What is the round of 16 in the NCAA tournament called?

source.notes: The term was trademarked by the Kentucky High School Athletic Association before the NCAA started using it.

questions.q: What is the round of 8 (regional finals) in the NCAA tournament called?
What is the round of 4 (semifinals) in the NCAA tournament called?

source.notes: Term first used by the NCAA officially in 1978.

questions.q: What is the term for predicting all 67 tournament outcomes (a perfect bracket)?

source.notes: Term popularized by ESPN's Joe Lunardi.

questions.q: What is the day called when the field is announced and brackets are revealed?

source.notes: Always the Sunday before the First Four games begin.

questions.q: What is the lowest seed ever to win the NCAA men's championship (1985)?

source.notes: Villanova upset top-seeded Georgetown in the final.

questions.q: Which network has aired the NCAA tournament since 1982 (now in partnership with Turner)?

source.notes: Joint deal with Turner since 2011 (TBS, TNT, truTV).

questions.q: What is the title of CBS's iconic post-final-game tournament montage?

source.notes: Song written by David Barrett in 1986; Luther Vandross's 2003 cover is the most famous version.

questions.q: Which Florida team won back-to-back NCAA championships in 2006 and 2007?

source.notes: Coached by Billy Donovan; led by Joakim Noah, Al Horford, and Corey Brewer.

questions.q: Which Duke star hit one of the most iconic shots in tournament history — the 1992 buzzer-beater against Kentucky?

source.notes: Laettner's turnaround jumper at the buzzer (East Regional Final, 104-103 OT).

questions.q: What major event caused the cancellation of the 2020 NCAA tournament?
In which state was the entire 2021 NCAA tournament held in a single bubble due to COVID?

source.notes: Most games at Bankers Life / Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis.

questions.q: Which university won the men's NCAA tournament in 2023?

source.notes: UConn beat San Diego State in the final. Repeated as champs in 2024.
